content.problemStatement:
Chef and his girlfriend went on a date. Chef took XXX dollars with him, and was quite sure that this would be enough to pay the bill. At the end, the waiter brought a bill of YYY dollars. Print `"YES"` if Chef has enough money to pay the bill, or `"NO"` if he has to borrow from his girlfriend and leave a bad impression on her.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line `"YES"` if Chef has enough money to pay the bill and `"NO"` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `"yEs"`, `"yes"`, `"Yes"` and `"YES"` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line "YES" if X ≥ Y, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: X=1, Y=1 → X≥Y so YES.
Test case 2: X=1, Y=2 → X<Y so NO.
Test case 3: X=2, Y=1 → X≥Y so YES.
Test case 4: X=50, Y=100 → X<Y so NO.
First pair 5≥3 → YES. Second pair 3<5 → NO. Third pair 10=10 → YES.

solution.approach: For each test case read X and Y and compare them. If X is greater than or equal to Y, Chef can pay the bill, so output YES; otherwise output NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int X,Y; scanf("%d %d",&X,&Y); if(X>=Y) printf("YES\n"); else printf("NO\n");} return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    idx = 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx]); y = int(data[idx+1]); idx += 2
        out.append('YES' if x >= y else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
